red_baseline.reason: T029 isolates deterministic generation, bundle isolation, host policy, and real stdio to T030.
red_baseline.summary: 10/10 cases fail only because the generator entrypoint is absent.

validation.summary: 10/10 packaging contracts pass; deterministic double-build, archives, digests, isolated real stdio, host separation, and Node prerequisite are proven.

The final full run passed every T030 and product contract but one existing random-port fixture timed out; its complete 5-test file passed immediately on focused rerun. One POSIX-only test remains skipped on Windows.

review.evidence: US3 generation remains deterministic, identity-bound, policy-free, and separate per host.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Packaging contracts and isolated official-SDK launches prove separate self-contained host artifacts with one compatibility identity and no policy-bearing host metadata.

recovery: Added ESM createRequire and embedded package/schema metadata after real bundle startup failures; replaced a Windows non-ASCII fs.cpSync no-op in the test fixture; synchronized the npm payload contract.
